name: Asian Breakfast Stir-Fry
instructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, bring the water and quinoa to a rolling boil over high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low, cover with a tight-fitting lid, and let simmer gently until the quinoa is tender and has absorbed all the water, about 15 to 20 minutes. Remove from heat and let stand covered.

2. While the quinoa cooks, prepare your vegetables. Roughly chop the carrot, broccoli, and onion, then pulse them in a food processor until finely chopped but not pureed, maintaining some texture.

3. Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium heat until it shimmers. Add the minced garlic and sauté, stirring constantly, until fragrant and just beginning to turn golden, about 1 minute. Be careful not to burn the garlic.

4. Add the chopped vegetables to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until the onions become translucent and the vegetables begin to soften, about 3 to 5 minutes. Add the kale and continue cooking, tossing everything together, until the kale wilts down, about 1 minute more.

5. Pour in the soy sauce and water, stirring to combine. Let the mixture cook for 5 more minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the liquid to reduce slightly. Add the shredded chicken and cooked quinoa to the vegetable mixture, stirring well to distribute everything evenly. Cook until everything is heated through, about 2 to 3 minutes.

6. Transfer the stir-fry mixture to serving plates. Lightly spray the same skillet with cooking spray and return it to medium heat. Crack the eggs into the skillet and cook them to your preference—sunny-side up, over-easy, or scrambled—about 3 to 5 minutes depending on your desired doneness.

7. Carefully place the cooked eggs on top of the stir-fry on each plate. If desired, drizzle with chile-garlic sauce, sprinkle with fresh cilantro leaves, and finish with a generous scattering of sesame seeds for added flavor and crunch. Serve immediately while hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Simmer quinoa in water until tender and fluffy.

2. Pulse vegetables in food processor until finely chopped.

3. Heat sesame oil, then sauté garlic until fragrant and golden.

4. Toss in vegetables and cook until softened, then wilt in the kale.

5. Add soy sauce and water, then stir in chicken and quinoa until heated.

6. Cook eggs to your liking in the same pan.

7. Top the stir-fry with eggs, garnish with sauce, cilantro, and sesame seeds, then dig in!
